description: Comprehensive taxonomy of life events and situations

events.name: graduation
events.description: Completing an educational program or degree
helpful_responses: Celebrate this achievement with me — what are you most proud of?
It's normal to feel both excited and scared about what's next.
What kind of support would feel helpful as you navigate this transition?
You've accomplished something significant. Take time to honor that.
helpful_questions: What felt most meaningful about your journey?
What are you looking forward to in this next chapter?
What fears are coming up for you?

events.name: job_loss
events.description: Being laid off, fired, or losing employment
helpful_responses: I'm so sorry you're going through this. Losing a job is genuinely hard.
Your worth is not defined by your employment status. You are still a capable, valuable person.
It's okay to feel whatever you're feeling right now — shock, anger, relief, fear. All of it is valid.
Let's talk about what practical support you might need right now.
helpful_questions: What's feeling hardest about this right now?
What kind of support would be most helpful?
What are your immediate concerns we can talk through?
things_to_avoid: Telling them it's a blessing in disguise
Only talking about next job search

events.name: divorce
events.description: Legal dissolution of marriage
helpful_responses: Divorce is a profound loss, even when it's the right decision. It's okay to grieve.
You're not a failure for choosing your wellbeing. Sometimes the bravest thing is letting go.
This is a major life transition. It's okay to take things one day at a time.
I hear how much pain you're in. What do you need most right now?
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of this process for you?
What would self-care look like today?
What kind of support do you have around you?
things_to_avoid: Making assumptions about the other person
Telling them it'll be fine quickly

events.name: moving_to_new_city
events.description: Relocating to a different city or country
helpful_responses: Moving somewhere new takes so much courage. How are you settling in?
It's completely normal to feel a mix of excitement and loneliness right now.
Building a new life takes time. Be patient with yourself.
helpful_questions: What drew you to this new place?
What feels most different from where you came from?
Have you found any spots that feel like 'yours' yet?
things_to_avoid: Telling them to just go out and meet people

events.name: bereavement
events.description: Loss of a loved one through death
helpful_responses: I'm so deeply sorry for your loss. There are no words that can make this right.
Grief is not a straight line. However you're feeling today is okay.
You don't have to be strong right now. You just have to be.
Tell me about them, if you want to. I'd love to hear what made them special.
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of today?
Would you like to share a memory of them?
How can I best support you right now?
things_to_avoid: Saying they're in a better place

events.name: promotion
events.description: Advancement to a higher position at work
helpful_responses: Congratulations! This is a real recognition of your abilities.
It's completely normal to feel both excited and terrified — that means you're growing.
What kind of support do you have in this new role?
helpful_questions: What part of this new role are you most excited about?
What feels most challenging about the transition?
How can you set yourself up for success?

events.name: serious_illness_diagnosis
events.description: Being diagnosed with a serious medical condition
helpful_responses: I hear how overwhelming this diagnosis must feel. You don't have to process it all at once.
It's okay to be scared. This is frightening news, and your feelings are valid.
You're not alone in this. What does your support system look like?
There's no right way to feel right now. Whatever comes up is okay.
helpful_questions: What's the most pressing concern on your mind right now?
Who in your life knows about this diagnosis?
What would feel most supportive in this moment?

events.name: pregnancy
events.description: Expecting a child
helpful_responses: This is such a big life change. How are you feeling about it?
It's completely normal to feel a mix of excitement and fear.
Your feelings — whatever they are — are valid. Many people feel ambivalent at times.
helpful_questions: What part of this journey feels most exciting to you?
What worries are coming up?
How can you take care of yourself through this?

events.name: infertility_struggle
events.description: Difficulty conceiving a child
helpful_responses: I'm so sorry you're going through this. Infertility is such a difficult journey.
It's okay to feel angry and sad. This is a real loss and it's painful.
You are not broken. Your worth is not tied to your ability to conceive.
It's normal to struggle with jealousy when others announce pregnancies. Be gentle with yourself.
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of this journey for you?
How are you and your partner navigating this together?
What would self-care look like for you right now?

events.name: retirement
events.description: Transition from working life to retirement
helpful_responses: Retirement is such a big transition — both liberating and challenging.
It's normal to feel lost without work structure. Be patient as you find your new rhythm.
This is a chance to discover parts of yourself that work may have overshadowed.
helpful_questions: What are you looking forward to in retirement?
What feels most uncertain about this transition?
What hobbies or interests are you excited to explore?
things_to_avoid: You'll love having all that free time

events.name: bullying
events.description: Repeated aggressive behavior targeting someone
helpful_responses: What you're experiencing is not okay. You deserve to feel safe and respected.
This is not your fault. Nothing about you justifies being bullied.
I believe you, and I'm sorry this is happening.
Let's talk about what options are available to keep you safe.
helpful_questions: Can you tell me more about what's been happening?
Who have you told about this?
What would make you feel safer right now?

events.name: academic_pressure
events.description: Stress from educational demands and expectations
helpful_responses: Academic pressure can feel crushing sometimes. What's weighing on you most?
Your grades don't define your worth. You are so much more than a number.
It's okay to ask for help. That's a sign of strength, not weakness.
helpful_questions: What subject or area is causing the most stress?
What would a more balanced schedule look like?
Who could you reach out to for academic support?

events.name: financial_hardship
events.description: Serious financial difficulty or poverty
helpful_responses: I hear how hard this is. Financial stress touches every part of life.
You are not your bank account. This situation doesn't define your worth.
Let's think about what resources might be available to help.
helpful_questions: What's the most pressing financial concern right now?
What support systems or resources have you explored?
How is this financial stress affecting your wellbeing?

events.name: self_discovery
events.description: Journey of understanding oneself more deeply
helpful_responses: Self-discovery is such a brave journey. It's okay if it feels uncomfortable at times.
You're allowed to grow and change. Who you were doesn't limit who you can become.
There's no timeline for knowing yourself. Be patient with the process.
helpful_questions: What parts of yourself are you exploring right now?
What feels freeing about this journey?
What feels scary or uncertain?
things_to_avoid: I always knew you were X

events.name: empty_nest
events.description: When children leave home for the first time
helpful_responses: This is such a bittersweet transition. It's a sign of good parenting that they're ready to go.
It's normal to feel both proud and heartbroken at the same time.
This is also an opportunity to rediscover yourself. What parts of you are ready to come forward?
helpful_questions: What are you feeling most strongly right now?
What might you want to explore for yourself now?
How are you and your partner adjusting to the change?

events.name: coming_out
events.description: Sharing one's LGBTQ+ identity with others
helpful_responses: Thank you for trusting me with this part of who you are.
Coming out is incredibly brave. Your identity is valid and beautiful.
There's no right or wrong way to be who you are. Your journey is yours.
You have the right to share your identity on your own terms and timeline.
helpful_questions: How are you feeling about this step?
What kind of support do you have right now?
What would feel safest for you right now?
things_to_avoid: Telling them who to tell or not tell

events.name: college_transition
events.description: Starting college or university
helpful_responses: Starting college is such a big step. How are the first few weeks going?
It's completely normal to feel homesick and excited at the same time.
Everyone else is also trying to make friends — you're not alone in that.
helpful_questions: What's been the best part so far?
What's been harder than you expected?
Have you found any groups or activities you're interested in?
things_to_avoid: These are the best years of your life

events.name: burnout_recovery
events.description: Recovering from chronic work or life stress exhaustion
helpful_responses: Burnout is real and it takes time to recover. Be patient with yourself.
Rest is not a reward for productivity — it's a necessity for wellbeing.
You can't pour from an empty cup. What would refuel you even 5 percent?
helpful_questions: What led to this point of burnout?
What would genuine rest look like for you?
What boundaries can you start putting in place?

events.name: addiction_recovery
events.description: Process of overcoming substance or behavioral addiction
helpful_responses: I'm proud of you for choosing recovery. That takes immense courage.
Recovery is not a straight line. Every step forward counts, even the small ones.
You are not your addiction. You are a person healing and growing.
What support do you have in place for this journey?
helpful_questions: What motivated you to seek recovery?
What's been the hardest part so far?
What coping strategies are helping you right now?

events.name: trauma_processing
events.description: Working through the impact of traumatic experiences
helpful_responses: I'm sorry you went through that. You didn't deserve what happened to you.
Healing from trauma takes time — sometimes years. There's no timeline you need to follow.
Your responses to trauma were survival mechanisms. They helped you get through.
You are not your trauma. You are a whole person who has experienced difficult things.
helpful_questions: What feels most important for you to talk about today?
How is your nervous system feeling right now?
What helps you feel safe and grounded?
things_to_avoid: You should be over it by now

events.name: career_change
events.description: Transitioning to a different career or field
helpful_responses: Career changes take courage. It's never too late to pursue what matters to you.
It's normal to feel like an imposter when starting something new. That will fade.
Your past experience isn't wasted — it gives you a unique perspective.
helpful_questions: What's drawing you to this new path?
What feels most scary about the change?
What skills from your current career will transfer?

events.name: having_first_child
events.description: Becoming a parent for the first time
helpful_responses: Becoming a parent changes everything. It's okay to feel both overwhelmed and in love.
There's no perfect parent. You're learning alongside your baby, and that's enough.
Taking care of yourself is part of taking care of your baby. What do you need right now?
helpful_questions: How are you really doing? (Not just how you think you should be doing)
What support do you have in this new chapter?
What's surprised you most about parenthood?

events.name: homelessness
events.description: Lack of stable, safe housing
helpful_responses: I'm so sorry you're in this situation. You deserve a safe place to be.
This is not your fault. Many people are one paycheck away from housing insecurity.
You are still a person worthy of dignity and respect, no matter your housing status.
helpful_questions: What's your biggest safety concern right now?
What resources have you connected with?
What would help most in this moment?
things_to_avoid: Assuming all homeless situations are the same

events.name: postpartum_depression
events.description: Depression after childbirth
helpful_responses: Postpartum depression is a medical condition, not a character flaw. You are not to blame.
Many mothers experience PPD. You are not alone, and you are not broken.
Getting help is not a sign of failure — it's a sign of strength and love for your baby.
Your baby needs a healthy mother, not a perfect one.
helpful_questions: When did these feelings start?
Have you talked to your doctor about how you're feeling?
What kind of support do you have right now?

events.name: loss_of_pet
events.description: Death of a beloved animal companion
helpful_responses: I'm so sorry for your loss. The love we share with animals is real and profound.
It's okay to grieve deeply for a pet. They were a member of your family.
Some people won't understand how much this hurts — but I do. Tell me about them.
helpful_questions: What was your favorite thing about them?
Would you like to share a memory?
How are you taking care of yourself through this?
things_to_avoid: You can always get another one

events.name: wedding_planning
events.description: Planning and preparing for marriage
helpful_responses: Wedding planning can be both beautiful and stressful. How are you managing?
Remember that the marriage matters more than the wedding day.
It's okay if everything isn't perfect. What matters is the commitment you're making.
helpful_questions: What part of wedding planning is bringing you joy?
What's causing the most stress?
How are you and your partner navigating decisions together?

events.name: caregiving_for_parent
events.description: Taking care of an aging or ill parent
helpful_responses: Caregiving for a parent is one of the hardest roles there is. It's okay to struggle with it.
You're doing the best you can. That is enough.
You deserve care too. Who is taking care of you while you take care of them?
It's normal to feel both love and resentment. Both feelings can coexist.
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of caregiving right now?
What support do you have in this role?
What would one moment of rest look like for you?
things_to_avoid: You're so lucky to have this time
Just put them in a home

events.name: breakup
events.description: Ending of a romantic relationship
helpful_responses: I'm sorry you're going through this. Breakups are painful, even when they're necessary.
Your feelings are valid — whether it's relief, sadness, or both.
Take all the time you need to heal. There's no timeline for grief.
You are whole and worthy of love, regardless of relationship status.
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part for you right now?
How are you taking care of yourself?
What support do you need from friends or family?

events.name: marriage
events.description: Entering into marriage or committed partnership
helpful_responses: Congratulations on your marriage! This is a beautiful commitment.
It's normal to feel both excited and nervous about this big step.
Remember that marriage is a journey — it takes work from both partners.
What matters most is the love and respect you share.
helpful_questions: What are you most looking forward to in marriage?
What concerns do you have about this transition?
How are you and your partner preparing for married life?
things_to_avoid: Just wait until the honeymoon phase ends

events.name: parenting
events.description: Raising children through various stages
helpful_responses: Parenting is one of the hardest jobs there is. You're doing great.
It's okay to feel overwhelmed — that's part of being a parent.
No parent is perfect. What matters is your love and effort.
Take care of yourself so you can take care of them.
helpful_questions: What's bringing you joy in parenting right now?
What feels most challenging?
How are you and your partner sharing responsibilities?

events.name: family_conflict
events.description: Disagreements or tensions within family relationships
helpful_responses: Family conflict is painful because it involves people we love.
It's okay to feel frustrated and hurt. Your feelings are valid.
Sometimes space is needed before resolution is possible.
What matters is how you move forward, not being right.
helpful_questions: What's at the core of this conflict for you?
How are you managing your own emotions in this?
What would a resolution look like for you?

events.name: abuse
events.description: Experiencing physical, emotional, or sexual harm from another person
helpful_responses: I believe you. This is not your fault.
You deserve to be safe and treated with respect.
It took courage to share this. I'm here for you.
There are resources available to help you.
helpful_questions: Are you safe right now?
What support do you have around you?
Would you like help connecting with resources?
things_to_avoid: What did you do to provoke it?

events.name: university_stress
events.description: Stress specific to university or college life
helpful_responses: University stress is real and common. You're not alone in feeling this way.
It's okay to ask for help — that's what services are there for.
Your mental health matters more than any grade.
Take it one semester at a time.
helpful_questions: What's causing the most stress right now?
What support services have you used?
How are you balancing academics with self-care?

events.name: debt
events.description: Struggling with significant debt or financial obligations
helpful_responses: Debt can feel crushing, but there are paths forward.
You're not defined by your financial situation.
Many people struggle with debt — you're not alone.
Let's explore options together.
helpful_questions: What's the biggest source of your financial stress?
What resources or support have you explored?
How is this affecting your daily life?

events.name: chronic_illness
events.description: Living with a long-term or ongoing health condition
helpful_responses: Living with a chronic illness is incredibly challenging. I see your strength.
It's okay to grieve the life you expected. Your feelings are valid.
You are more than your illness. Your identity is multifaceted.
What does a good day look like for you?
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of managing your condition?
How are you adapting to your new normal?
What support do you have in place?

events.name: disability
events.description: Living with a physical, sensory, or cognitive disability
helpful_responses: Your experience is valid, and your needs matter.
You are not defined by your disability. You are a whole person.
It's okay to feel frustrated with barriers. Those barriers are real.
What accommodations or support would make a difference for you?
helpful_questions: What are the biggest challenges you face daily?
How can I best support you?
What helps you feel empowered?
things_to_avoid: I know exactly how you feel

events.name: grief_loss
events.description: Experiencing grief from any significant loss
helpful_responses: Grief is love with nowhere to go. Your pain makes sense.
There's no right way to grieve. Your process is your own.
I'm here with you in this, for as long as you need.
What you're feeling is normal, even if it doesn't feel like it.
helpful_questions: What's the hardest part of today?
What would bring you even a moment of comfort?
How can I honor what you're going through?
things_to_avoid: You should be over it by now

events.name: loneliness_social
events.description: Feeling disconnected or lacking meaningful social connections
helpful_responses: Loneliness is a universal human experience. You're not alone in feeling this way.
It's okay to feel lonely even when surrounded by people.
Connection takes time. Be patient with yourself as you build it.
What would meaningful connection look like for you?
helpful_questions: What kind of connection are you craving most?
What small steps could you take toward community?
How does loneliness show up for you?
things_to_avoid: Just go out and meet people

events.name: social_isolation
events.description: Being physically or socially cut off from others
helpful_responses: Isolation can feel crushing. Your need for connection is valid.
Even small connections can make a difference. What feels possible?
You deserve to be seen and heard, even when it's hard.
Let's explore ways to gently reconnect.
helpful_questions: What's keeping you isolated right now?
What would help bridge the gap?
How is this isolation affecting your wellbeing?

events.name: immigration
events.description: Moving to a new country or cultural context
helpful_responses: Immigrating takes incredible courage. How are you adjusting?
It's normal to feel torn between two cultures. That's part of the journey.
Your heritage is valuable. You can honor it while adapting.
What support would help you feel more at home here?
helpful_questions: What's been the biggest challenge in adapting?
What do you miss most from home?
What has surprised you positively?
things_to_avoid: If you don't like it, go back

events.name: identity_exploration
events.description: Questioning or exploring aspects of personal identity
helpful_responses: Exploring your identity is brave and important work.
There's no 'right' answer. Your journey is yours to discover.
It's okay to take your time. Identity unfolds gradually.
What feels authentic to you right now?
helpful_questions: What aspects of yourself are you exploring?
What feels freeing or exciting about this process?
What support would help you feel safe to explore?

events.name: aging
events.description: Navigating the physical, social, and emotional changes of growing older
helpful_responses: Aging brings both loss and wisdom. Both are real and valid.
Your life experience is valuable. What you've lived matters.
It's okay to grieve what you've lost while appreciating what remains.
How are you finding meaning in this stage of life?
helpful_questions: What feels most challenging about aging?
What wisdom have you gained?
How can you stay connected and purposeful?
things_to_avoid: You're only as old as you feel
At least you lived a full life

events.name: natural_disaster
events.description: Experiencing a natural event like earthquake, hurricane, flood, or wildfire
helpful_responses: I'm so sorry you went through that. Natural disasters are terrifying and traumatic.
Your feelings of fear and loss are completely valid.
You've survived something incredibly difficult. That takes strength.
What support do you need right now to start rebuilding?
helpful_questions: How are you and your loved ones doing?
What's your biggest concern right now?
What resources or support have you been able to access?

events.name: community_violence
events.description: Experiencing or witnessing violence in one's community
helpful_responses: No one should have to live in fear in their own community.
Your feelings of fear and anger are understandable and valid.
This violence is not your fault. You deserve to feel safe.
What would help you feel more secure right now?
helpful_questions: How are you coping with what you've experienced?
What support do you have in your community?
What would you need to feel safer?
